message:
feat(fcp-core): add MeshNodeConfig with documented heartbeat / peer / lease defaults

Implements bead flywheel_connectors-1aerk ("MeshNodeConfig
defaults"). Introduces a shared mesh-node runtime config struct so
mesh callers reach for one canonical defaults bundle instead of
re-declaring 30-second heartbeats / 32-peer caps / 5-minute lease
TTLs at every site. Closes the gap where the documented defaults
were comments rather than first-class constants.

Production change (crates/fcp-core/src/protocol.rs):

- Three new public constants:
  * DEFAULT_MESH_NODE_HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL = Duration::from_secs(30)
  * DEFAULT_MESH_NODE_MAX_PEERS         = 32
  * DEFAULT_MESH_NODE_LEASE_TTL          = Duration::from_secs(300)
  Each constant carries the doc string identifying it as the
  documented default, so a future change to the defaults shows up
  as a deliberate edit on the constant rather than an ad-hoc
  literal somewhere in the codebase.
- New `pub struct MeshNodeConfig` with three Duration / usize
  fields wrapping those constants:
    pub heartbeat_interval: Duration
    pub max_peers:          usize
    pub lease_ttl:          Duration
  Derives Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq so it slots into
  config-comparison hot paths. A `pub const fn new()` constructor
  returns the documented-defaults instance; callers that want
  alternates set the fields explicitly post-construction.
- `time::Duration` is added to the existing std::time use group at
  the top of the file.

Pinning test (crates/fcp-core/tests/mesh_node_config_defaults.rs,
new):

- Asserts that MeshNodeConfig::new() and ::default() produce
  byte-identical instances and that each field equals the
  corresponding DEFAULT_* constant. Adding a new field to the
  struct or shifting a default value will fail this test loudly,
  preventing a "31-second heartbeat slipped in" silent regression.

Crate-root re-exports added so `MeshNodeConfig`, the three
DEFAULT_MESH_NODE_* constants, and any associated trait impls are
all reachable through `fcp_core::*`.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
